title: ericameria ericoides var. pachylepis
intro: Ericameria ericoides var. pachylepis, also known as Bigelow's goldenrod, is a shrub-like perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of essential oils and sesquiterpene lactones. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and pain, and may also exhibit antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects. Bigelow's goldenrod can be prepared as an herbal tea, salve, or infusion, and is often used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
